title: 1.1: Reports the SMTP status message returned when an email is forwarded to an existing alias (e.g., 250 Message accepted for delivery)
1.2: Reports the SMTP status message returned when an email is forwarded to a non-matching alias (e.g., 550 SL E515 Email not exist).
1.3: Reports the SL Message-ID value stored in the database during the forward operation (e.g., None or null).
1.4: Reports that the original Message-ID from the incoming email is preserved in the forwarded message.
1.5: Reports the display name format in the transformed From header (e.g., sender name followed by sanitized email such as "John Doe - sender at external-domain.com").
1.6: Reports the reply-email address format used in the From header email portion (e.g., pattern like sender_at_domain_com_[randomstring]@sl.local).
1.7: Reports an actual Contact record ID from the trace execution (e.g., Contact ID: 6).
1.8: Reports an actual Contact record timestamp from the trace execution (e.g., Created At: 2026-01-02 10:05:03.294846+00:00).
1.9: Reports an actual EmailLog record ID from the trace execution (e.g., EmailLog ID: 6).
1.10: Reports an actual EmailLog record timestamp from the trace execution (e.g., Created At: 2026-01-02 10:05:03.342469+00:00).
